tasks.title: CREA benchmark ingestion (Tangshan blast-furnace operating rate)
tasks.description: Ingest the Tangshan blast-furnace operating-rate benchmark from CREA's public WIND-sourced CSV: fetch, version a dated snapshot, parse (skip metadata headers, select the column by exact name), clean 0.00->NA, and emit a tidy parquet series plus retained auxiliary steel series. SCOPE NOTE: the spec also designed a validation/correlation module, but implementing it was premature because the NO2 predictor (attribution stage) does not exist yet to validate against. Validation was reverted to a scaffold and deferred to NOX-003 (post-attribution); the design remains recorded in the spec for reuse.

review.note: Delivered scope: benchmark ingestion only. Validation modules reverted on 2026-06-13 as premature (no predictor yet).

tasks.title: TROPOMI NO2 acquisition + AOI subset + QA (via openEO)
tasks.description: Acquire Sentinel-5P/TROPOMI L2 tropospheric NO2 over the Tangshan AOI for the TROPOMI era (2019-01 -> present) using openEO server-side spatial subsetting (not full-granule download: ~3,734 overpasses x ~609 MB = ~2.2 TB avoided -> ~0.2-1 GB kept). Use OFFL + RPRO for a version-consistent record and track the processor version per observation; apply the QA filter (qa_value >= 0.75 default); retain tropospheric NO2 column, qa, cloud fraction, averaging kernel, geolocation, precision per overpass; track per-period valid-coverage fraction; idempotent/resumable. Includes a verification step: on clear-sky high-NO2 days, render the NO2 field over the AOI with facility locations overlaid (and an optional optical thumbnail) to confirm enhancements co-locate with the steel sub-clusters. Gridding/compositing is NOX-002b; attribution is NOX-003.

review.note: 29 tests, ruff clean. Live-verified 2026-06-13 with developer CDSE creds: SENTINEL_5P_L2 collection confirmed (1 band/request, time dim 't', no qa_value/kernel -> gridded; cloud-based filtering); fetched 2023-06-01..06 over the AOI and rendered 2023-06-04 -> NO2 elevated over the central steel corridor (diffuse/regional, consistent with sub-pixel + BTH background). Averaging kernel deferred to a future L2 path (NOX-003). See open Q1.

tasks.title: TROPOMI NO2 gridding + temporal compositing (analysis-ready cube)
tasks.description: Assemble the per-overpass AOI NO2 from NOX-002a (already on a regular ~0.05x0.035 deg openEO grid) into a time-stacked, temporally-composited, coverage-screened analysis-ready cube in data/derived: harmonize overpasses onto a common grid, composite to a configured frequency (weekly default; monthly optional) by mean over valid cells, track per-cell and per-period valid-coverage, and apply the minimum-coverage threshold (cloud decision 2026-06-13) without interpolation. Because openEO already delivers a gridded product, this is harmonization + compositing, NOT swath oversampling (Beirle-style fine oversampling needs the raw L2 path, deferred to NOX-003). Also emits an interim AOI-mean weekly NO2 series (spatial mean, explicitly pre-attribution) matching the validation predictor contract, to enable a first end-to-end smoke of the validation engine. NO2-derived outputs are gitignored (not published). Resolution: native ~5 km (decision A, 2026-06-13) — adequate for the cluster signal.

review.note: 36 tests, ruff clean. Ran on the real (partial) store: weekly cube (232 slots) + interim AOI-mean series (76 rows), gitignored. Re-run 'noxus grid' after the full fetch completes; tune coverage thresholds (Q2) then.

tasks.title: Steel-sector NO2 activity signal + validation vs CREA (relative-index attribution, v1)
tasks.description: Derive a steel-sector activity signal for Tangshan from the gridded NO2 cube (NOX-002b) and validate it against the CREA blast-furnace operating-rate benchmark (NOX-001). v1 uses the RELATIVE-INDEX / cluster paradigm (Kim 2023), NOT flux divergence (Beirle, deferred to NOX-005). Pipeline: sample cells on a footprint around the steel facilities (data/derived/tangshan_steel_facilities.csv) rather than a flat AOI mean; subtract/normalize a regional background; apply meteorological normalization by ingesting ERA5 wind/PBL and regressing it out (Mao 2025 / Li 2024 approach) -- decided 2026-06-13 to mitigate the dominant weekly-ventilation confounder; deseasonalize (YoY/double-differencing or anchor normalization); emit a relative activity index; validate vs CREA with Pearson r + p-values and lead/lag tests; this revives the validation/correlation module deferred from NOX-001. REQUIREMENTS to treat explicitly: heating/non-heating confounder (steel NO2 share 42.5%->29.1%, SciRep 2024), policy/curtailment confounder (Li 2024), empirical sign verification for Tangshan (Montgomery 2018), attributable-fraction cap (~30-43%), relative (not absolute) signal due to TROPOMI low bias (-23..-33%). Success bar: r ~ 0.5-0.75 on deseasonalized series; honest NULL is an acceptable outcome (Morris&Zhang 2019). NO2-derived outputs gitignored.

review.note: Approved-with-nits across phase 1 (T1-T4), phase 2 (T5-T14) and the post-review ERA5->CDS delta; all nits (N1-N3, D1-D2) resolved. 90 tests, ruff clean. ERA5 source = Copernicus CDS (server-side AOI subset, per-year requests; ARCO rejected). Deseason default = yoy (double-diff demoted). First real end-to-end run produced an HONEST NEAR-NULL: raw levels negative (retrofit decoupling, Li 2024); after yoy detrending monthly r~0.19 (peak +0.29 at lag +6 mo), below the 0.5-0.75 bar -> valid reported null (Montgomery/Morris&Zhang). See docs/preliminary-results.html.

tasks.follow_ups: Optional v2 / follow-ups (not blocking done): model the retrofit trend explicitly (Li 2024 activity/policy/meteo decomposition); flux-divergence point-source attribution (NOX-005); public curtailment calendar (Q6a); re-run the full chain after the complete TROPOMI fetch and tune footprint/coverage thresholds.

tasks.title: Steel emission-intensity (decoupling) model + full-series re-run
tasks.description: Additive increment on NOX-003. NOX-003 produced a two-layer result: footprint NO2 correlates NEGATIVELY with the CREA blast-furnace operating rate in levels (monthly r~-0.35) and only weakly positive after blind yoy detrending (r~0.19, below the 0.5-0.75 bar). The negative-levels relationship is the signature of DECOUPLING (emission intensity / NOx-per-tonne falls secularly faster than activity rises; retrofits/policy, Li 2024). Blind difference filters under-remove (yoy) or erase (double-diff) that trend. This task models emission intensity EXPLICITLY: fit a smoothness-controlled secular trend s(t) on the meteo-normalised footprint signal and define the activity proxy as the residual (Li 2024 activity/intensity/meteo decomposition). Smoothness is selected by out-of-sample / blocked time-series cross-validation on the NO2 series ALONE -- never against the benchmark (anti-p-hacking, NFR-102) -- and a smoothness-sensitivity sweep is reported so any signal is shown robust, not an artefact of one smoothing choice. The secular trend is emitted as a reportable diagnostic (the decoupling), not discarded; the report states both the negative levels relationship and the residual relationship with CIs. Second half: the DEFERRED full-series re-run -- once the complete TROPOMI fetch + rebuilt cube exist, re-run the chain on the longer series and tune footprint radius + coverage thresholds on the complete coverage distribution, resolving NOX-003 Q1/Q5 and NOX-002b Q2. ADDITIVE ONLY: all existing deseason methods (yoy/stl/yoy-double-diff/none) and CLI behaviour are retained for replicability; intensity-model is a new selectable method. Relative-index discipline, attributable-fraction cap, honest-null and flux-divergence-deferred (NOX-005) all carry over unchanged. The full-series result is the go/no-go gate for NOX-005.

review.note: Approve T1-T9 (core method, dual-sign decoupling report, gates, figures). 107 tests, ruff clean. Anti-p-hacking verified: fit_intensity_trend takes no benchmark arg, CV scores on NO2 alone, planted-null sweep flat across df. Real partial-cube result: levels r=-0.35 (decoupling confirmed), intensity-model residual monthly r~0.14 (peak 0.19), comparable to yoy, below the 0.5-0.75 bar -> honest near-null persists; methodological gain is removing the difference-filter degree of freedom. T10 (full-series re-run + coverage tuning) is DATA-GATED on the complete TROPOMI fetch (cube partial); the cube-completeness gate (ERR-103) and radius sweep are in place. T11 docs partly done (preliminary-results.html, decision log, history, project-map); methods note optional.

tasks.follow_ups: Data-gated / non-blocking: (T10) full-series re-run + footprint-radius/coverage-threshold tuning after the complete TROPOMI fetch + 'noxus grid' rebuild (closes NOX-003 Q1/Q5, NOX-002b Q2) -> the full-series number is the go/no-go gate for NOX-005; (T11) optional standalone methods note on the intensity model; change-point/segmented trend for step-change retrofits (EDGE-103); curtailment calendar (Q6a).

tasks.title: Steel NO2 event catalyst (production-event detection -> market lead)
tasks.description: PARADIGM SHIFT (developer-directed 2026-06-13): stop using the cluster NO2 as a continuous tracker of the BF operating rate (NOX-003/003.1 showed it is a weak below-bar tracker) and use it as a CATALYST / EVENT MARKER. Detect discrete production events -- sharp surges/drops in cluster NO2 (curtailment start/end, sudden shutdowns/restarts) -- on the NOX-003.1 intensity-detrended + meteo-normalised residual, and test whether they (a) LEAD the official production print and (b) precede moves in steel-exposed markets. Why it fits: a catalyst lives in the tails (large deviations), so a low overall r is irrelevant; Tangshan events are discrete/sharp; and the slow decoupling trend that killed the continuous correlation does not create sudden jumps (removed once via the NOX-003.1 residual). Pipeline: event detection (robust anomaly + change-point, coverage-screened, STRENGTHENED meteo control = ventilation index + stagnation flag so a still-air spike is not an event -- the cheap in-framework lever for 'better weather correlation', NOT flux-divergence which is deferred NOX-005) -> ground-truth production events (CREA BF-rate jumps COMBINED WITH a public curtailment calendar) -> event-study match (precision/recall/lead time, not Pearson r) -> market event-study (cumulative abnormal returns around events) for THREE instrument families: Chinese ferrous futures (SHFE rebar/DCE iron ore/coking coal), global miners (BHP/RIO/VALE), and a China steel ETF/equities -> catalyst report. DISCIPLINE: strict no-look-ahead (causal baselines; trade at next session after overpass+latency), multiple-testing transparency (fix defaults before viewing returns), public/free data only (Chinese ferrous futures lack a clean free API -> Q1: lead with miners+ETF, ferrous as best-effort exchange-settlement snapshot or deferred), honest NULL is a valid outcome (Morris&Zhang 2019). NO2-derived events + market price snapshots gitignored. Statistical power benefits from the full TROPOMI fetch (NOX-003.1 T10).

review.note: Full catalyst implemented (events/groundtruth/market/study/report + CLI). 124 tests, ruff clean. Deep-review focus holds: NO-LOOK-AHEAD (causal expanding median/MAD; guard test; trade at overpass+latency) and MULTIPLE-TESTING (defaults fixed pre-returns; n_tests recorded) verified; ventilation/stagnation meteo screen rejects weather spikes; coverage screen; confirmed-vs-unconfirmed split; public/free yfinance only (ferrous deferred Q1); NO2 events + price snapshots gitignored. Real partial-cube run (live yfinance): HONEST NULL -- 25 NO2 events, 4/47 production matched (precision 0.16, recall 0.09, median lead -2 d), market CARs straddle zero. Machinery correct; event-marker weak on partial data -> full-fetch re-run + tuning is the decisive test.

tasks.follow_ups: Non-blocking: re-run after the full TROPOMI fetch (NOX-003.1 T10) + tune z-threshold/match-window/study-window on the longer series; assemble the public curtailment calendar (Q4); add Chinese ferrous futures via exchange daily-settlement snapshot (Q1); optional event-timeline / CAR / precision-recall figures + a docs/ result note. The full-series catalyst result also informs the NOX-005 (flux-divergence) go/no-go.

tasks.title: Flux-divergence point-source attribution (Beirle) -- deferred phase
tasks.description: DEFERRED future phase (decided 2026-06-13, phased-hybrid). Point-source NOx attribution via NO2 flux divergence (Beirle 2021/2023): flux = TVCD x ERA5 wind, divergence isolates the local source term, with chemical-loss and plume-height corrections (PAL product, v2). Requires a RAW L2 swath acquisition path (NOX-002a delivered the openEO gridded product, not raw swath needed for fine oversampling) plus ERA5 winds. Known to resolve poorly in high-background / complex-wind regions like industrial NE China -- expected to be hard at Tangshan; that risk is the reason it is a separate phase after the relative-index v1 (NOX-003) establishes whether any signal exists.
tasks.blocked_reason: Needs a raw L2 swath acquisition path + ERA5; sequenced after NOX-003 establishes signal feasibility.
